data.title: Irys announces IRYS token economics: 20% initial circulating supply, 8% allocated for airdrops and future incentives.
data.description: Irys, a programmable data chain, announced its IRYS token economics: The total supply of IRYS tokens is 10 billion, with an initial circulating supply of 20%. The allocation is as follows: Ecosystem 30%, Foundation 9.9%, Airdrop and Incentives 8%, Liquidity and Launch Partners 8%, Team and Advisors 18.8%, and Investors 25.3%. Team and investor tokens will be locked for the first year. A 2% inflation reward will be distributed to validators annually, halving every four years; 50% of execution fees and 95% of regular storage fees will be burned.

data.title: BitMart launches the "Contract Supernova" event, with a prize of 1 BTC.
data.description: According to official news, BitMart has launched the "Contract Supernova" event. During the event, users can earn chances to win prizes by completing tasks such as registration, advanced verification, net contract deposits, and trading volume. The prize pool includes rewards such as 1 BTC, 1 ETH, SOL, DOGE, and various tiers of USDT. The event has a guaranteed minimum reward to ensure that all participants receive a prize. Participants earn a chance to win a prize by completing their first contract trade with 1 USDT. Additionally, the event offers tiered tasks ranging from 100 USDT to 200,000 USDT, allowing users to participate according to their trading needs.

data.title: Opinion: Bitcoin is likely to find support at $81,800 and $74,800; a drop to $74,800 may mark the bottom of this bear market.
data.description: Trader Banmu Xia posted that Bitcoin currently has two high-probability bottoming levels: 81,800 and 74,800 (Coinbase price), as well as the range between these two prices. If it reaches 74,800, it could very well be the lowest point of this bear market.

data.title: A whale sold off some of its WBTC and liquidated its ETH holdings, incurring a loss of $26.348 million over four months.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684xtpa), a whale "cleared out its ETH holdings and sold some WBTC." In the past 8 hours, it sold 250 WBTC ($21.17 million) at an average price of $84,710, incurring a loss of $7.948 million. Its ETH holdings were liquidated in batches between November 17th and November 21st, ultimately selling 17,497 ETH ($52.41 million) at an average price of $2,994.9. The previously established 13,316 ETH position suffered a loss of $18.4 million. The whale's WBTC and ETH accumulated at the peak in August and September of this year have lost $26.348 million in less than four months, with the remaining 1,310 WBTC still showing a floating loss of $40.71 million.

data.title: Opinion: The recent decline in the crypto market was mainly driven by retail investors selling off Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs.
data.description: JPMorgan analysts say the recent pullback in the crypto market—particularly exacerbated by Bitcoin falling below the bank's estimated $94,000 production cost/support level—was primarily driven by retail selling of Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs, rather than crypto-native traders. "While crypto-native investors contributed to the market pullback in October through significant deleveraging (particularly in perpetual contracts), this deleveraging in perpetual contracts appears to have stabilized in November," wrote Nikolaos Panigirtzoglou, Managing Director at JPMorgan, and his team in a report on Wednesday. "Instead, the main force driving the continued pullback in the crypto market in November has been non-crypto investors, particularly retail investors who entered the crypto market through Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs." Analysts noted that retail investors have withdrawn approximately $4 billion from Bitcoin and Ethereum spot ETFs so far this month, exceeding the record net outflows of February. This behavior contrasts sharply with retail investor flows in the stock market. Retail investors poured approximately $96 billion into equity ETFs (including leveraged products) in November—if this pace continues until the end of the month, the total will approach $160 billion, comparable to levels seen in September and October. They point out that retail investors have previously exhibited similar “divergent behavior”: aggressively buying stocks while simultaneously selling crypto ETFs in a few months (only February, March, and now November this year). This suggests that retail investors still view crypto assets and stocks as two separate asset baskets, even though both are risky assets.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: If Strategy's stock is removed from the index, it may face billions of dollars in capital outflow.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase stated in a report on Thursday that if global financial index provider MSCI removes Bitcoin "vault giant" Strategy (MSTR) from its stock indices, related capital outflows could reach as high as $2.8 billion; if other exchanges and index compilers follow suit, the total outflow could reach $11.6 billion. The analysis points out that MSTR's recent stock price decline—coupled with its overall weak performance this year—stems more from market concerns about its potential removal from MSCI and multiple indices such as the Nasdaq 100 and Russell 1000, rather than from a decline in Bitcoin's own price. "It is precisely because of these indices' inclusion that Bitcoin exposure has indirectly seeped into the portfolios of retail and institutional investors," the analysts wrote. "However, with MSCI now considering removing MicroStrategy and other companies with predominantly digital asset holdings from its stock indices, this previous indirect penetration could be reversed." MSCI is evaluating a proposal to exclude companies whose primary business is holding Bitcoin or other crypto assets, and where such assets account for more than 50% of their balance sheets. MSCI stated last month that this "consultation" will continue until the end of this year, with a final decision expected by January 15th.

data.title: Re7 Labs Engages Legal Firm for Asset Recovery Amid Financial Dispute
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Re7 Labs has announced via Twitter that it has hired the law firm Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman to investigate and provide legal advice on formal options, including asset recovery. This move follows multiple unsuccessful attempts to contact Stream Finance and Stables Labs for satisfactory responses. Legal notices have been sent to the relevant parties, demanding repayment and evidence preservation. Currently, Morpho Worldchain's sdeUSD position has been fully restored, reducing the treasury's risk exposure by over 50%.

Previously, Foresight News reported that Re7 Labs released a report detailing the impact of issues with stablecoin protocols like Stream, with total losses amounting to $27 million.

data.title: Bitwise XRP ETF Debuts on NYSE Following SEC Approval
data.description:
According to Odaily, on November 20, Eastern Time, the Bitwise XRP ETF (ticker: XRP) was officially listed on the New York Stock Exchange after receiving approval from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).

Data from SoSoValue indicates that on November 20, the total net inflow for XRP spot ETFs was $118 million. On its first day of trading, the Bitwise XRP ETF saw a net inflow of $105 million, with a trading volume of $26.41 million and a total net asset value of $108 million.

The Canary XRPC ETF experienced a single-day net inflow of $12.8 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $305 million.

As of the time of reporting, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs stands at $385 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.32%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $411 million.

The Bitwise XRP ETF allows for cash or physical redemption and has a management fee rate of 0.34%.

data.title: Tom Lee: The deleveraging cycle in the crypto market may be nearing its end.
data.description: In an interview with CNBC, BitMine Chairman Tom Lee stated that the current weakness in the crypto market is highly similar to the crash of October 10th, when a stablecoin pricing error triggered the largest liquidation in history, resulting in nearly 2 million accounts being wiped out and liquidity drying up instantly. Lee pointed out that such deleveraging cycles typically last about 8 weeks, and we are currently in the 6th week, suggesting the market may be nearing the end of its correction.

data.title: Bitwise XRP ETF launched, with US XRP spot ETFs seeing a net inflow of $118 million in a single day.
data.description: On November 20th, Eastern Time, the Bitwise XRP ETF (ticker symbol XRP) officially listed on the NYSE after receiving SEC approval. According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (November 20th, Eastern Time), the total net inflow into XRP spot ETFs was $118 million. Specifically, the Bitwise XRP ETF saw a net inflow of $105 million on its first day of trading, with a trading volume of $26.41 million and a total net asset value of $108 million. The Canary XRPC ETF saw a single-day net inflow of $12.8 million, with a historical total net inflow of $305 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs is $385 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 0.32%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $411 million. The Bitwise XRP ETF supports cash or in-kind redemption and has a management fee of 0.34%.

data.title: Alliance Co-Founder Reiterates Caution on Cryptocurrency Market
data.description:
According to Odaily, Alliance co-founder @QwQiao has recently reiterated his warning about the cryptocurrency market, expressing strong agreement with his previous predictions. He had previously cautioned that the next bear market could be more severe than most anticipate, primarily due to the influx of uninformed investors purchasing digital asset trading platforms (DAT) and ETF products.

He believes this situation "never ends well" and forecasts that the market might need to undergo a roughly 50% correction before these investors exit their positions. Only then can the market establish a solid foundation and resume its supercycle growth. Although he did not specify a timeline, his reaffirmation indicates his firm stance on this prediction.

data.title: XRP New Today: XRP Dips 6% to Hover Around $2 Amid Whale Sell-Off
data.description: Key TakeawaysXRP price tumbles 6% to hover around $2.00, extending its multi-day weakness and reflecting bearish momentumWhale distribution accelerates, with 200M XRP offloaded in 48 hours, creating strong selling pressureBitwise XRP ETF closes 7% lower on debut, adding to ETF-driven volatilityXRP Declines As Whale Sell-Off IntensifiesXRP slipped 4.75% to $2.02 in the last 24 hours, underperforming the broader crypto market’s –4.69% move. Blockchain data shows whales distributed nearly 200M XRP (roughly $404M) over the past two days, adding significant selling pressure that outweighed buyer demand.This activity follows XRP’s 33% 90-day decline, signaling that larger holders may be reducing exposure amid continued downside risk. Rising exchange reserves further reinforce a cautious tone as more tokens move into liquid markets, a typical precursor to heightened volatility. ETF Debut Adds Volatility Instead of Boosting SentimentThe Bitwise XRP ETF, launched on Nov. 20, recorded $21.7M in turnover but closed the session 7% lower, reflecting a soft reception relative to the sector. Meanwhile, Canary Capital’s XRPC ETF, with $268M AUM, continues to dominate interest, raising questions about Bitwise’s ability to attract institutional participation.Weakness across traditional markets added to the pressure, with the Dow Jones falling –0.84%. Market sentiment remains “extreme fear” with a reading of 15, while rising Bitcoin dominance (58.16%) signals a broader shift into perceived safer crypto assets. Technical Structure Shows Further Downside RiskXRP broke below the $2.04 pivot and is now retesting the critical $2.00 Fibonacci (78.6%) support. The RSI at 33 signals oversold conditions but shows no bullish divergence, suggesting the downtrend may persist.A confirmed breakdown below $2.00 would extend the descending triangle pattern that formed after XRP fell from the $3 psychological level. The pattern’s structure points toward a measured move to $1.55, representing a possible 25% decline from current levels.The pattern confirmation occurred when XRP slid below $2.20 earlier this week. Historical analysis also warned of potential declines toward $1.61, reinforcing the current bearish setup.Source: TradingViewAdding to the overhead pressure, nearly 3.23B XRP were acquired between $2.38–$2.40, creating a supply cluster aligned marginally below the 100-day SMA, a stiff resistance zone that continues to cap upward momentum. Outlook: Can XRP Defend The $2.00 Support?The key question is whether XRP can hold $2.00, a psychologically and technically important level. A sustained break could expose the token to the $1.98 range and potentially toward the $1.55 triangle target.
